title: Leucanthemum margaritae
taxonomy: Leucanthemum margaritae is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Leucanthemum, which includes other species of daisies commonly found in Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The exact classification of Leucanthemum margaritae is not as widely documented as other species in the genus, but it is generally categorized as a member of the plant family Asteraceae, subfamily Asteroideae. Further classification details may require more specific botanical information. More research is needed to accurately determine the complete classification of Leucanthemum margaritae.
uses: Leucanthemum margaritae has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region, where it is believed to have various medicinal properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. In some parts of the world, Leucanthemum margaritae is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, due to its potential antispasmodic and astringent properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Leucanthemum margaritae in treating these conditions. However, its traditional use suggests potential applications in natural remedies.

intro: Leucanthemum margaritae, also known as Margaret's daisy, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region.
